original_hypothesis.title: FeeRateModel getFeeRate passes msg.sender (caller) as 'pool' parameter, enabling misbinding in fee implementations
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: FeeRateModel.getFeeRate forwards IFeeRateImpl.getFeeRate(msg.sender, trader, lpFeeRate). When called from a MagicLP pool, msg.sender is the pool (good). But any other caller (off-chain quote, Router-like contract, or attacker contract) will be treated as 'pool' by fee implementations, potentially returning incorrect fee rates or reverting. This can break integrations that query fees off-pool, and can be exploited to craft misleading quotes or to cause DoS in calling contexts that assume getFeeRate is stable.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is operationally relevant because Router/MagicLP query paths and UI quoting may call FeeRateModel directly. If fee implementation uses 'pool' for allowlists/tiers and callers are not pools, results diverge.
deployment_assumptions: FeeRateModel.implementation uses the `pool` parameter for logic (tiers, allowlists, per-pool config) rather than ignoring it (FeeRateModelImpl ignores it).
External callers besides MagicLP call FeeRateModel.getFeeRate for quoting or validation.
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Deployment script sets FeeRateModelImpl which ignores pool/trader; however FeeRateModel supports swapping implementation by owner.
preconditions: Implementation contract uses pool identity to compute fees or enforce permissions

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Fee quoting behavior depends on caller identity; can desync off-chain quotes vs on-chain execution
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: after fee implementation update or during integrator misuse

operational_questions: Do any current or planned fee implementations rely on the `pool` parameter?
Do UIs/SDKs call FeeRateModel directly for quotes (not via pool query functions)?
Are there monitoring checks ensuring FeeRateModelImpl remains the implementation?
validation_requirements: Trace all on-chain call sites to FeeRateModel.getFeeRate
Review future fee model roadmaps and candidate implementations
Create a fee impl that enforces pool allowlist and observe failures from non-pool callers

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Rely on integrator that calls FeeRateModel directly for quotes
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Cause quote/execution divergence or DoS by changing caller context (e.g., via contract wrapper)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Users trade with incorrect expectations; operational failures occur
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Depends on integration patterns and fee implementation

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: quote instability, integration breakage, potential mispriced trades if integrator uses wrong fee rates
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: medium (requires integration fixes or fee model constraints)
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: fee quoting and any external systems depending on FeeRateModel

investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: sudden quote deviations after fee implementation changes

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
